description: Fix needsAttributeObservation in dom.ts to detect attribute-backed CSS pseudo-class selectors, and add missing unit tests for findLocalStorageEntry, getAuthCache, setAuthCache, and clearAuthCache in storage.test.ts.

userStories.title: Fix needsAttributeObservation to cover attribute-backed pseudo-class selectors
userStories.description: The needsAttributeObservation function in dom.ts only checks for '[' and '.' in a CSS selector to decide whether to enable attributes:true in the MutationObserver. This misses pseudo-class selectors like :checked, :disabled, :required, :optional, :read-only, :read-write, :default, :valid, :invalid, :in-range, :out-of-range, and :placeholder-shown — which all reflect HTML attribute changes. A plugin calling waitForSelector('input:required') on an already-present input that gets setAttribute('required','') added will time out instead of resolving because the observer never fires for attribute mutations. Fix the function to also return true for selectors containing these pseudo-classes, then add tests to dom.test.ts verifying the fix.
acceptanceCriteria: waitForSelector resolves when an existing element's attribute is set making it match a pseudo-class selector like 'input:required' (i.e., via element.setAttribute('required', ''))
waitForSelector resolves when an existing element's attribute is set making it match ':checked', ':disabled', or ':enabled'
waitForSelectorRemoval resolves when an existing element's attribute is removed making it no longer match ':disabled' (i.e., via element.removeAttribute('disabled'))
Selectors without '[', '.', or attribute-backed pseudo-classes still set attributes:false in the observer (no regression for class/attribute selectors)
needsAttributeObservation returns true for selectors containing :checked, :disabled, :required, :optional, :read-only, :read-write, :enabled, :default
needsAttributeObservation returns false for plain tag selectors like 'div', 'span', 'input#myId'
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/plugin-sdk/src/dom.ts (fix needsAttributeObservation)
- platform/plugin-sdk/src/dom.test.ts (add tests for the fixed behavior)

Context:
The function needsAttributeObservation (line 9 of dom.ts) determines whether to enable attributes:true in MutationObserver options when calling waitForSelector and waitForSelectorRemoval. It's used in both functions at the observer.observe() call:

  observer.observe(document.documentElement, {
    childList: true,
    subtree: true,
    attributes: needsAttributeObservation(selector),
  });

Current implementation (line 9):
  const needsAttributeObservation = (selector: string): boolean => selector.includes('[') || selector.includes('.');

This correctly handles:
- '[disabled]' or 'input[type="text"]' — contains '['
- '.my-class' or 'div.active' — contains '.'

But it misses pseudo-classes that CSS maps to HTML attribute presence:
- ':checked'       — reflects checked attribute (also property, but setAttribute fires mutation)
- ':disabled'      — reflects disabled attribute
- ':enabled'       — reflects absence of disabled attribute
- ':required'      — reflects required attribute
- ':optional'      — reflects absence of required attribute
- ':read-only'     — reflects readonly attribute
- ':read-write'    — reflects absence of readonly attribute
- ':default'       — reflects default-checked state (HTML attribute)
- ':valid'/':invalid'   — reflects validity state (can be attribute-driven)
- ':in-range'/':out-of-range' — reflects min/max attributes
- ':placeholder-shown' — reflects placeholder attribute

For an already-existing input without 'required', a MCP tool calling:
  await waitForSelector('input:required')
...expects it to resolve when 'required' is later added via:
  inputEl.setAttribute('required', '')
But with attributes:false, the observer never fires for attribute mutations, so the promise times out.

Changes:

Replace the current needsAttributeObservation function with one that also detects these pseudo-classes. A concise approach using a regex:

  const ATTRIBUTE_PSEUDO_RE = /:(checked|disabled|enabled|required|optional|read-only|read-write|default|valid|invalid|in-range|out-of-range|placeholder-shown)/;

  const needsAttributeObservation = (selector: string): boolean =>
    selector.includes('[') || selector.includes('.') || ATTRIBUTE_PSEUDO_RE.test(selector);

This regex is intentionally conservative — it only covers pseudo-classes whose state is directly reflected in a named HTML attribute (as opposed to :hover, :focus, :active which depend on user interaction, not attribute mutations).

Tests to add in dom.test.ts (inside the existing 'waitForSelector' describe block or a new sub-block):

1. 'resolves when attribute is set on existing element making it match :required pseudo-class':
   - Add an input without required to body
   - Start waitForSelector('input:required')
   - In queueMicrotask, call input.setAttribute('required', '')
   - Expect the promise to resolve with the input element

2. 'resolves when attribute is set on existing element making it match :disabled pseudo-class':
   - Similar pattern with :disabled and setAttribute('disabled', '')

3. Add similar test in 'waitForSelectorRemoval' describe block:
   - 'resolves when attribute is removed from existing element making it no longer match :disabled'
   - Add a disabled button, start waitForSelectorRemoval('button:disabled')
   - In queueMicrotask, call button.removeAttribute('disabled')
   - Expect promise to resolve

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the function signature or export status of needsAttributeObservation
- Do NOT touch waitForSelector or waitForSelectorRemoval logic — only needsAttributeObservation changes
- The regex should NOT match :hover, :focus, :active, :visited, :nth-child, etc. — these don't reflect named attributes
- The existing tests for '[' and '.' selectors must continue to pass
- The 'attributes: true' test in observeDOM describe block tests a different code path (observeDOM options) — do not confuse it with waitForSelector behavior

userStories.title: Add missing unit tests for findLocalStorageEntry, getAuthCache, setAuthCache, clearAuthCache
userStories.description: storage.test.ts has zero tests for findLocalStorageEntry, getAuthCache, setAuthCache, and clearAuthCache — all four are exported functions with non-trivial behavior. findLocalStorageEntry has iframe fallback logic identical in complexity to getLocalStorage (which is well-tested), and the auth cache functions have edge cases around initialization and the undefined-vs-delete behavior of clearAuthCache. Add comprehensive tests for all four functions.
acceptanceCriteria: findLocalStorageEntry has tests for: finding a matching entry, returning null when no entry matches, returning null when localStorage throws (SecurityError), using the iframe fallback when localStorage is undefined (same mock pattern as getLocalStorage iframe tests)
getAuthCache has tests for: reading a value from an initialized cache, returning null when namespace is not found, returning null when __openTabs is not set, returning null when tokenCache is not set
setAuthCache has tests for: writing a value to tokenCache, initializing __openTabs and tokenCache when absent, overwriting an existing value
clearAuthCache has tests for: the cleared namespace returns null from getAuthCache after clearing, no-op when namespace doesn't exist, no-op when cache is not initialized
clearAuthCache test verifies the key is set to undefined (not deleted) — i.e., namespace key still exists in tokenCache object with value undefined after clearing
